name: Z-stagger split-massing silhouette
description: Overall silhouette whose footprint is two offset rectangular masses overlapping at one corner, reading as a Z (or stagger). The shift breaks up the bulk, creates a sheltered re-entrant entry corner and two outdoor terraces on opposite diagonal corners. Common in modern split-level houses and pavilions that step with a sloping site. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides the two masses.
placement_rules: Footprint is two rectangles offset diagonally, overlapping at a shared corner.
The overlap (20-40%) holds the shared circulation joining the two masses.
The two exposed diagonal corners stay open as terraces.
Each mass is >= 6 in both XZ so rooms fit.
Place the entrance in the sheltered re-entrant corner.

modern.notes: Two white volumes sliding past each other; flat roofs, glazed re-entrant entry.

minimalist.notes: Pared-back staggered boxes; single material, sharp shadow line at the offset.
